branches.effect_on_first_success: Requires additional manual steps to create CloudFormation stack and grant permissions
branches.path: AWS-specific cluster setup with CloudFormation stack and IAM role

branches.condition: Cloud provider is GCP or Azure
branches.effect_on_first_success: Potential differences in provisioning steps and permissions required
branches.path: Cluster setup process varies; details not fully documented in quickstart

candidate_paths.name: CLI-based bundle installation
candidate_paths.relationship_to_primary_path: Alternative to web dashboard deployment; focuses on installing pre-built bundles via CLI rather than deploying source code from GitHub.
candidate_paths.selection_basis: Official quickstart documentation includes a CLI-based path to install and manage bundles as an alternative to the web dashboard deployment flow.

details: Run platform-specific install script for MacOS, Linux, or Windows

steps.action: Install a bundle using porter install command
details: Run porter install with bundle reference, e.g. ghcr.io/getporter/examples/porter-hello:v0.2.0
steps.failure_or_wait: Error if bundle not found or install fails

details: Run porter list to see installed bundles
steps.success_signal: porter list output with bundle metadata

details: Run porter show with installation name
steps.success_signal: porter show output with status and logs

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: Your application is now live and will automatically redeploy whenever you push to your selected branch

documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: Developer successfully deploys their application and accesses it live on a public URL
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: Application is live and accessible at *.onporter.run URL shown in the Porter dashboard
documented_first_success.official_milestone: Application is deployed and accessible via a public URL on Porter
documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: This milestone marks the first time the developer's application is running live on Porter and accessible publicly, indicating successful completion of the onboarding deployment flow.

assumed_prior_state: Developer has an active cloud provider account (AWS, GCP, or Azure) with admin or owner permissions
Developer has a deployable application with a start command or Dockerfile, pushed to GitHub or container registry
entry_point.developer_intent: Deploy their first application on Porter

friction_gates.description: Creating a Porter account and project requires valid project name and successful account creation
Cloud provider account must have admin or owner permissions to create Kubernetes clusters and IAM roles
For AWS, user must acknowledge IAM resource creation in CloudFormation and create the stack
User must review and accept estimated monthly costs for infrastructure provisioning
Connecting GitHub requires authorizing the Porter GitHub App and selecting repository access
User must merge the GitHub Actions PR to trigger deployment

prerequisites.requirement: Active cloud provider account (AWS, GCP, or Azure) with admin or owner permissions to provision infrastructure
Deployable application with a start command or Dockerfile, pushed to a GitHub repository or container registry

primary_path.action: Create a Porter account and project
details: Navigate to dashboard.porter.run
Create an account
Click Create Project and enter a project name (1-25 chars, lowercase letters, numbers, hyphens)
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Error if project name invalid or account creation fails

primary_path.action: Select cloud provider to provision infrastructure
details: Choose AWS, GCP, or Azure
Reuse previously connected cloud credentials if available
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Error if credentials invalid or provider not supported
primary_path.success_signal: Cloud provider selection confirmed in UI

primary_path.action: Set up Kubernetes cluster in selected cloud account
details: For AWS, enter AWS Account ID
Create CloudFormation stack to provision IAM role
Review estimated monthly costs and accept
Customize or accept default cluster configuration
Provision cluster
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Wait for provisioning; error if IAM role creation fails or quota limits exceeded
primary_path.input: AWS Account ID and cluster configuration

details: Click Create Application
Connect GitHub account by authorizing Porter GitHub App
Select repository and branch to deploy
Review detected applications and configure build/start command/port if needed
Click Deploy to create GitHub Actions PR
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Wait for user to merge PR; error if GitHub authorization fails
primary_path.success_signal: Toast notification with View PR button and warning banner with Merge PR link

primary_path.action: Merge GitHub Actions PR to trigger build and deployment
details: Merge the PR created by Porter in GitHub repository
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Wait for build and deployment to complete; failure if build or deployment errors occur
primary_path.output: GitHub Actions workflow runs to build and deploy application
primary_path.success_signal: Build logs and deployment progress visible in Porter dashboard

primary_path.action: View running application and access public URL
details: Monitor deployment progress and logs in Porter dashboard
Click the automatically provisioned public URL (*.onporter.run) to access the live application
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Wait if deployment is still in progress; error if deployment fails
primary_path.output: Live application accessible at public URL

sections_used: Step 1: Sign up and create a project
sources.title: Quickstart

alternatives_considered.name: Porter CLI
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: CLI is used mainly for bundle installation and management after initial setup; the first-mile onboarding focuses on the web dashboard for project creation and app deployment.

surface.name: Porter Web Dashboard and CLI
surface.selection_basis: The official quickstart documentation primarily guides users through the web dashboard for initial setup and deployment, with CLI usage documented for bundle installation and management.

uncertainties.impact: Potential differences in friction gates, permissions, and provisioning steps could affect onboarding experience and time to first success.
uncertainties.question: What are the detailed cluster setup steps and requirements for GCP and Azure cloud providers?
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The official quickstart documentation details AWS cluster setup extensively but only mentions GCP and Azure without detailed steps.

uncertainties.impact: Knowing exact time expectations helps set user expectations and measure onboarding efficiency.
uncertainties.question: Is there an officially documented time estimate for time to first success from account creation to live application?
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The documentation states 'Deploy your first application to Porter in minutes' but does not provide a precise or vendor-claimed time.
